title: rhamnus californica subsp. ursina
intro: Rhamnus californica subsp. ursina, also known as Mountain Mahonia or Oregon Grape, is a shrub native to the western United States and is characterized by its spiny, evergreen foliage and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin problems, due to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Mountain Mahonia include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve liver function, and support the immune system. It is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, with herbal preparations typically involving the use of the plant's roots and leaves.
